title: U Mass - Chronic drug delivery

Chronic drug delivery may be used to examine intermediate to long-term effects of selected drug on obesity, insulin resistance, and metabolism.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":180275,"guid":"41A6C58A341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Osmotic pump","mol_weight":0,"sku":"1007D","url":"http://www.alzet.com/products/guide_to_use/pump_selection.html","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":522,"name":"Alzet","link":"http://www.alzet.com/company/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Anesthetize mice with an intraperitoneal injection of ketamine (100 mg/kg body weight) and xylazine (10 mg/kg body weight).
Shave hair at the incision site on the back.
Make an incision (~0.5 cm) using sterilized scalpel between the scapulae.
Subcutaneously insert an Alzet mouse osmotic pump containing drug or placebo.
Suture or close the incision site using sterilized staples.
Administer ketoprofen to minimize pain and house mice individually.
creator: Lili Liang
